published_prior_art.cite: LeeSunSunTaylor2016Polyhedral + TibshiraniTaylorLockhartTibshirani2016Sequential: routine fixed-catalogue selection-geometry application, not IDA-specific

LeeSunSunTaylor2016Polyhedral gives pivots once Ay<=b is explicit; ChangGuoMalinsky2026PostSelectionCausalDiscovery and GraduZrnicWangJordan2025ValidInference cover post-causal-discovery inference but not deduplicated IDA lower-bound reports
LeeSunSunTaylor2016Polyhedral: undercoverage of naive post-selection intervals is standard; IDA two-branch lower-bound specialization appears not directly published

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline promises an explicit polyhedral tangent certificate (A_s,b_s), but §9 only computes the fixed-catalogue signed-minimum fragment, not the graph-screen plus duplicate-partition certificate from §6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Flagship comparator chain cites MaathuisKalischBuhlmann2009IDA, GuoPerkovic2021MinimalEnumeration, ChangGuoMalinsky2026PostSelectionCausalDiscovery, and GraduZrnicWangJordan2025ValidInference without location-level anchors to the exact open gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The result is positioned as a Lee-style certificate plus IDA specialization, but it does not state a frontier between two published post-causal-discovery inference procedures or promote to a fully exhibited generic class.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The undercoverage statement is a qualitative two-branch failure claim; it does not name a new threshold, coverage-gap functional, or other focal mathematical object.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The full graph-screen/duplicate-partition generic certificate has no non-corner worked witness; the only exhibits cover the already-fixed catalogue/minimum fragment.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The affine inequalities are algebraically wrong: with a=sigma*theta_j>=0, |theta_j|<=|theta_k| is a<=|theta_k|, a disjunction over theta_k signs, not the simultaneous constraints a<=theta_k and a<=-theta_k.

soundness_flags.label: Exhibit 9.1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed nonempty witness (a,b)=(0.2,-0.4) violates A_min*theta<=0 because a-b=0.6>0; the stated conditions b>=a>=0 and b<=-a are incompatible except at zero.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The conjecture claims existence of a polyhedral tangent certificate under Assumption 6, but Assumption 6 already assumes the explicit computable (A_s,b_s), making the main burden assumed rather than stated as a result.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Coverage target is ambiguous: the text does not specify whether the naive interval is meant to cover the selected branch effect, the signed minimum contrast, or the nonsmooth lower-bound score L_s.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Once Assumption 6 supplies A_sZ<=b_s, the pivot statement is essentially Lee-style selective inference plus definition substitution; the nontrivial certificate derivation is not in the theorem hypotheses.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Theorem 1 with the correct geometry: either condition on the full competitor sign pattern and add sign constraints for every branch, or state the signed-minimum event as a finite union of polyhedra rather than one affine system.
Move the existence/computability of (A_s,b_s) out of Assumption 6 and into Conjecture 1; §7 should only define the Gaussian screen, duplicate rule, and selection state primitives.
Add a full §9 exhibit for a small CPDAG/MPDAG where the CI-screen inequalities, duplicate partition, branch catalogue, signed minimum, and truncation limits are all computed from primitives.
Sharpen Conjecture 2 into a named coverage-gap functional with a concrete numerical parameter point and specify the target parameter of the naive and selective intervals.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version; the fixed-catalogue signed-minimum theorem and Exhibit 9.1 must be algebraically repaired before any novelty-tier review can proceed.
